mapping.a_element: the coupling constant ξ tuned to select inflationary regimes
mapping.b_element: the free-laser detuning and feedback-phase parameters swept to select soliton states

mapping.a_element: the extended constant-roll regime that exists only for suitable ξ
mapping.b_element: the soliton-number-dependent existence ranges (including exclusive single-soliton regions) in parameter space

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic notion of a nonlinear system whose accessible states depend on control parameters; the actual machinery differs. Paper A uses slow/constant-roll inflaton dynamics (potential force vs coupling-induced friction in an expanding universe) to compute CMB observables n_s and r, whereas Paper B performs bifurcation analysis of dissipative Kerr solitons under self-injection locking to map existence/stability ranges — different governing equations with no role-for-role correspondence.
